Container.Name: actual-sync
Container.Overview: Automated bank transaction synchronization for Actual Budget. Runs scheduled syncs across multiple budget instances (including encrypted budgets), with a web dashboard, Prometheus metrics, multi-channel notifications (Email/Telegram/Slack/Discord), retry logic, and health monitoring. On first start it seeds a config.example.json template into the Config path and exits; fill it in, rename it to config.json, and restart. See the project README for the schema.
Container.Description: Automated bank transaction synchronization for Actual Budget with multi-server support, scheduling, encrypted-budget support, a web dashboard, Prometheus metrics, and multi-channel notifications. First start: if the Config directory has no config.json, the container writes a config.example.json template there and exits. Fill in each server's url / password / syncId (Sync ID is in Actual Budget → open the budget → Settings → Advanced → "Sync ID"), rename it to config.json, and restart. The health/dashboard/metrics endpoint is served on container port 3000 (/health, /ready, /metrics, /dashboard). Keep healthCheck.host as "0.0.0.0" in config.json. Do NOT set it to your server's LAN IP, or the dashboard will be unreachable in bridge mode.

Config.Name: Config
Config.Description: Directory containing config.json (validated against the project schema at startup).

Config.Name: Data
Config.Description: Persistent data: SQLite sync history and per-server Actual Budget data directories.

Config.Name: PUID
Config.Description: User ID the service runs as. On Unraid use 99 (nobody) so it can write the appdata directories.

Config.Name: PGID
Config.Description: Group ID the service runs as. On Unraid use 100 (users).

Config.Name: Timezone
Config.Description: Container timezone used for scheduling and log timestamps, e.g. America/New_York.

Config.Name: Node Environment
Config.Description: Node.js runtime environment. Leave as production unless debugging.
